prompt:
Use case: stylized-concept
Asset type: project-bound RPG world atlas map, first batch pass, landscape orientation
Primary request: D&D 5e / Forgotten Realms inspired high fantasy world map, dual-continent terrain type.
Scene/backdrop: overhead birds-eye atlas view with two major continents separated by uneven seas and narrow straits.
Subject: two asymmetric major continents, not a simple vertical split; use a diagonal S-shaped ocean channel with offset landmasses, inland seas, peninsulas, gulf systems, capes, bays, island-studded straits, broken coasts, river deltas, and realistic curved shorelines.
Style/medium: illustrated fantasy world map, cartographic parchment style, Tolkien-esque high fantasy atlas, digital fantasy concept art, highly detailed illustration, vibrant but readable RPG sourcebook quality.
Composition/framing: wide 16:9 landscape map, both continents fully visible, diagonal/S-shaped separation, generous ocean border, continent-scale geography.
Lighting/mood: heroic high fantasy with ancient ruins, frontier towns, scattered dungeon entrances, winding rivers, coastal harbors, tiny glowing rune stones, distant dragon silhouettes used sparingly.
Color palette: aged parchment and blue-green seas, with diverse forests, deserts, wetlands, tundra, alpine ranges, river basins, and coastal regions.
Materials/textures: aged parchment, engraved ink coastlines, hand-painted mountains and forests, fine atlas detail.
Constraints: no text labels, no letters, no legend, no compass text, no watermark, no UI, no characters or portraits. Avoid mirrored symmetry and avoid a clean vertical division.
